feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: useOneAsMany leaving out one node in target
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I have this source structure-

<Orders>
<Order>
<OrderNumber>ORD001</OrderNumber>
<CustomerID>CUST001</CustomerID>
<Item>
<SKU>SKU1</SKU>
<Qty>2</Qty>
</Item>
<Item>
<SKU>SKU2</SKU>
<Qty>5</Qty>
</Item>
</Order>
<Order>
<OrderNumber>ORD002</OrderNumber>
<CustomerID>CUST002</CustomerID>
<Item>
<SKU>SKU3</SKU>
<Qty>1</Qty>
</Item>
</Order>
</Orders>

that I need to map to the following target

<OrderLines>
<Line>
<OrderNum>ORD001</OrderNum>
<Customer>CUST001</Customer>
<SKU>SKU1</SKU>
<Qty>2</Qty>
</Line>
<Line>
<OrderNum>ORD001</OrderNum>
<Customer>CUST001</Customer>
<SKU>SKU2</SKU>
<Qty>5</Qty>
</Line>
<Line>
<OrderNum>ORD002</OrderNum>
<Customer>CUST002</Customer>
<SKU>SKU3</SKU>
<Qty>1</Qty>
</Line>
</OrderLines>

I am using useOneAsMany in the graphical mapping to achieve this. In will attach screenshots of the mapping. The <Line> is mapped to the <Item>. But the output is missing the final <Line>, this one-

<Line>
<OrderNum>ORD002</OrderNum>
<Customer>CUST002</Customer>
<SKU>SKU3</SKU>
<Qty>1</Qty>
</Line>

I am not quite sure what is going wrong. I am fairly new to using node functions. Please help me out. Thank You.

entry.title: Coupa API for Image scan, PDF read failing to convert the binary data to PDF
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I am trying to fetch a PDF from Coupa using API call using the Coupa Adapter. The API call provides me a Binary encoded file with UTF-8 format. I am able to create a PDF but this comes as a blank. I have referred most of the blogs to convert the binary to byte and to base64 string and so on but none of it resolves this issue. 

Any suggestions are appreciated.

entry.title: AzureStorage Fileshare Storage dynamic file name based on property
entry.content:
iflow receives multiple files and based on the file names I required to post the files to respective folder path of AzureStorage using FileShare for which I could use the router option, but when writing the file's dynamically based on the file name property is not working and getting exception on my receiver connector

/filesharepoc/Inbound/ReceiveFiles/${property.sourceFilename}

java.lang.StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: String index out of range: -1

but if I maintained the target file name as : /filesharepoc/Inbound/ReceiveFiles/test.txt which works

entry.title: I am simulating a jms queue using 2 iflows in SAP integration suite
entry.content:
I am simulating a jms queue using 2 iflows in SAP integration suite, one will send the data in a xml say ten records, which will have general spiltter that will split into single record and send to jms queue through sender adapter to second iflow, which will process and send the data to  an API http://123.133.1XX.111:5000/create_event through http adapter , I tested that by stopping the http://123.133.1XX.111:5000 server and test whether the data 10 records are there in jms queue  and is retrying.

The HTTP call with request reply is failing as the target system is  down (http://123.133.1XX.111:5000), but the data is not in the jms queue, it is only coming when there is duplicate datastore error or any error in the iflow steps , external call failure of http is not making the 10 records go in jms queue, I am achieving that by forcing  a error from a groovy from the exception sub process, like this
def errorMessage = message.getProperty("CamelExceptionCaught")?.toString()

if (errorMessage?.contains("Connection refused") ||
errorMessage?.contains("Read timed out") ||
errorMessage?.contains("500")) {
message.setProperty("sendToJMS", true);
throw new RuntimeException("Forced retry due to external system down");
} else {
message.setProperty("sendToJMS", false);
}

 

Is this the normal behaviour, please advise.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Load balancer for multiple SAP integration suite tenant
entry.content: Hi Experts, We are currently facing performance bottlenecks with our SAP Integration Suite tenant. We are exploring options to optimize performance or implement disaster recovery by provisioning additional instances. We came across a three-year-old SAP Community post Maximum Number of Messages for SAP Cloud Platform Integration indicating that Autoscaler Service support for SAP Integration Suite was on the roadmap. but not clear if this functionality has been implemented, If so, is there any guidance or documentation on how to configure the Autoscaler Service with SAP Integration Suite to enable automatic scaling based on workload or to support multi-instance disaster recovery. If this feature is not yet available, could you recommend alternative methods to address performance bottlenecks or achieve disaster recovery for SAP Integration Suite on BTP? For exam, is there any solution about the load balancer implemented for multiple CPI tenant regarding the external requests control? Any answer will be warm welcome. thanks in advance. Regards, Grady

entry.title: SAP Free tier limitations for Integration suite
entry.content:
Hi,

Referring to the SAP Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2903776.

Need few information's regarding the Free Tier Service Plan for SAP Cloud Integration.

1. How many interfaces can be developed in the Free Tier Service Plan?

2. Does the Free Tier support Best Effort Communication mode (Synchronous Communication)?

3. What is the message volume that is permitted in the free tier service plan?

4. What are the other limitations in using the free tier service plan ?

Regards,

Senthil

entry.title: custom headers are not working in monitoring with wildcard charcters in sap cpi
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have a requirement to store the salesforce ID values for each Salesorg, I have used addCustomHeaders function to store the values.

Since i get many records from salesforce all the ID values are stroed in CSV, during message monitoring i am unable to retrieve the message based on single ID value.

I tried giving * after the ID value, but still it was not showing the message with this search .
if i give the entire value i am able to see the message

can you help me how i could search with one ID if i have many ID values stored in custom header


Regards,
Saikumar

entry.title: Problem in a Flow between the GeneralSplitter appliance and the Aggregator
entry.content:
Hello dears,
I have a problem in a Flow between the GeneralSplitter appliance and the Aggregator,
there are two messages:

1.- In the Aggregator
GeneralSplitter may not pass XML message to Aggregator,Aggregator supports XML only

2.-At Flow Level
The Process requiere a transaction because aggregator is used. Set transacction handling to Requiered for JDBC


The message you are sending between the GeneralSplitter and the Aggregator are these three XML


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Inventory>
<Car>
<Id>1</Id>
<Brand>Renault</Brand>
<Model>Captur</Model>
<Year>2021</Year>
<Color>Azul</Color>
</Car>
</Inventory>

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Inventory>
<Car>
<Id>8</Id>
<Brand>Renault</Brand>
<Model>Prado</Model>
<Year>2021</Year>
<Color>Negro</Color>
</Car>
</Inventory>


<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Inventory>
<Car>
<Id>3</Id>
<Brand>Renault</Brand>
<Model>Prado</Model>
<Year>2021</Year>
<Color>Azul</Color>
</Car>
</Inventory>


Any help, thank you very much in advance Manos.
Regards,
Roberto Bahamondes Flores

entry.title: XMl to JSON conversion in sap cpi
entry.content:
Hello all

Has anybody tried to build custom XML to JSON conversion using jettison library , because Jettison is being used in SAP PO, Hence as part of migration we have to generate exactly same output as sap PO.

As it is a big XML hence JSON its very hard to handle each field to control the type .

 

Regards

Sandeep

entry.title: Local Integration Process Clarification
entry.content:
Dear All,

If we have a flow like below.

Main Integration Process (MIP)  -->  Local Integration Process 1 (LIP1)  --> LIP2

If I set any header in a groovy in LIP2 will this be available in LIP1 and MIP? My understanding is the headers are global in scope and all steps in an iFlow should be able to read and set them. I tried but LIP2 headers are not visible in LIP1. I also tried to define them in LIP1 first and set them in LIP2 but that also didn't help to read them in LIP1/MIP.

Also, what about the exception handling in LIP2 and LIP1? What is the best practice here? Is there any limitation if we have nested processes like this and is there any limit on them?

entry.title: App "API Business Hub Experience" is not accessible from "SAP Integration Suite" for my trial accont
entry.content:
Hi

According to chapter in course "Developing with SAP Integration Suite" https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite/creating-a-product_fc942de6-ff7b-4263-bd2b-f33fe481bc3d
One shall be able to see and access app/ecosystem "API Business Hub Experience".

But that is not visible for me.

The AuthGroup-rolecollections are in place for my user as I see it:

  • AuthGroup.ContentAuthor
  • AuthGroup.Site.Admin
  • AuthGroup.Content.Admin
  • AuthGroup.APIPortalRegistration
  • AuthGroup.API.ApplicationDeveloper
  • AuthGroup.API.Admin

Anyone else have same issue? Is something missing in my BTP setup perhaps?

 

Expected to see this when hovering over "Ecosystem" in SAP Integration Suite:

This is what I see instead:

 

Overview of instances in my SAP BTP Cockpit:

 

/Regards

Joakim

entry.title: Currency Conversion using Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi all. Today I will explain the use case of currency conversion that I have made using Integration Suite. Below I have attached the image of the Integration Flow that I have created:

In the Currency Parameter setting, we have set the 3 parameters : one for storing the currency in which we will insert our amount (say USD), second one for storing the target currency (INR), and the third one is for storing the amount which we want to convert to the target currency. Also, we set our APIKey here. Next we Request Reply to an api, say fastFOREX, for fetching the base currency and the target currency/currencies rate(s). Then we use the script Amount conversion to convert the given amount to target currency amount. Then we formulate our final message in Payload Creation that we want to be sent to the target mail ids using mail adapter.

Here's a sample mail that one will get after deploying it:

If you want to change the currencies you can do the same by changing the toCurrency and fromCurrency properties in the 'Currency Parameter setting' Content Modifier. 

Hope you like it.

#btp cpi

entry.title: Using SAP CPI Mail Adapter in iFlow
entry.content:
Hi All,

📌 Introduction

In SAP CPI (Cloud Integration), the Mail Adapter for Gmail plays a crucial role in automating email notifications and integrating email functionality into business processes. It allows seamless communication via SMTP, ensuring secure transmission of emails within integration flows.

Below is the image of the Integration Flow I created:

📌 Step 1: Content Modifier for Email Body

I have used a Content Modifier named Mail_body to define the email content dynamically using HTML code for styling. This ensures structured email formatting and supports customization.

📌 Step 2: Configuring the Mail Adapter

  • The Mail Adapter is configured using an SMTP connection to communicate with the Gmail server.

  • An Authentication Credential is created under Security Material to ensure secure email transmission and prevent unauthorized access.

  • Under the Processing tab, I have defined the mail attributes, including:

    • Recipient details (To, CC, BCC)

    • Subject line

    • Body Mime-Type set as "Text/HTML" for styling the email body using HTML.

📌 Step 3: Sample Email Output

Once deployed, here’s a sample email that users will receive: (Attach an example email screenshot here.)

📌 Step 4: Using Exchange Properties for Dynamic Content

To store dynamic email content (mail attributes), we use exchange properties in the Content Modifier. This allows dynamic values like recipient email, transaction details, and timestamps to be populated during runtime.

 

 

Conclusion

In this blog, we explored how to: ✅ Set up the Mail Adapter in an iFlow. ✅ Configure SMTP authentication for secure email transmission. ✅ Format HTML-based email bodies for structured messages. ✅ Optimize integration with exchange properties for dynamic content.

By applying these best practices, enterprises can enhance email reliability, security, and customization in their CPI workflows. With continuous enhancements in SAP Integration Suite, email-based automation will remain a powerful tool for business process efficiency.

If you have any questions or insights, feel free to share your thoughts in the comments!

entry.title: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodically from S/4HANA with Dynamic Plant/S.Loc
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement where a SAP CPI interface needs to be triggered once or twice daily. This interface performs the following:

--Accepts Plant and Storage Location as input.

--Fetches product data using an OData API in S/4HANA and some additional info from other APIs.

--Sends the final data to a target system.

What we initially considered:

Timer-based iFlow in CPI, with plant and storage location maintained as externalized parameters in a content modifier.

----Rejected by the team due to manual effort involved in changing parameters each time we want to trigger for different plant/storage location.

SAP BTP Job Scheduler: Allows periodic triggering and could call a CPI endpoint with required parameters. Currently in discussion. If selected, the job would pass Plant and Storage Location as part of the payload to CPI.

S/4HANA-triggered interface via SOAP/IDoc: S/4 can periodically send data (plant and storage location) via a custom ABAP job, which triggers a CPI endpoint using SOAP or sends an IDoc. This seems promising, but I would like to know what’s more standard/recommended.

Constraints:

We cannot use custom Z-tables in S/4HANA to manage or store configuration.

CPI as a public API for third-party to call isn’t suitable, as the source system must be S/4 or CI.

Event Mesh is not applicable here, since there is no real-time change or event-based trigger. It’s purely a periodic push.

My Questions:

1) Between BTP Job Scheduler and S/4HANA SOAP/IDoc push, which would be more recommended and scalable?

2) Are there any other better or more standard options to periodically trigger a CPI interface with dynamic parameters like plant and storage location?

entry.title: Partner Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We would like to continue development with Integration Suite for longer than 90 days and would like to purchase the service as cheaply as possible as the free service plan will remove the instance in 90 days.
I found a service plan called Partner Integration Suite in the Commercial Model: Pay-Go for BTP Test Demo Dev in the Discovery Center, but it does not describe the services offered.

SAP Discovery Center Service - Integration Suite

Please tell me about the Partner Integration Suite.

Best regards.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - Message Metric Question?
entry.content:
Hello,

we have an iFlow in Integration Suite which transfers binary data from ECC to a Cloud System.
The binary date is transported in the body of the message.
SAP Note 2942344 describes the measurement of a message:
"......If an integration message is larger than 250 kilobytes, any amount in excess of 250 kilobytes will be charged as one additional message for each 250 kilobytes or portion thereof......"

If we want to transfer a file with 30MB as binary data SAP measures 120 messages (30.000 kilobytes / 250 kilobytes)! Is this correct?

Is there anywhere in iflows or the integration suite where you can see how large a transmitted message was?

Thanks

entry.title: Unable to Publish Product in SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using trial account but I am unable to publish my products.

 

 

The roles assignment is as mentioned below -

I can also see the devloper hub Icon in the switcher and developer role is also registered in the developer hub.

I already checked few SAP Notes and added the roles accordingly, but nothing worked for me.

Please advise what is wrong here?

Thanks,

Nidhi

entry.title: Is the Cloud Connector feature available for Kafka System with SAP Integration Suite ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm exploring integration options for Kafka System using SAP Integration Suite. Can anyone confirm if the cloud connector feature is available for connecting Kafka On-Premise with SAP Integration Suite using Kafka adapter. If so, could you share official documentation links.

Thank you in advance for your insights!

entry.title: IS: Cannot create iFlow in Test tenant. Error while loading the details of existing integration flow
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have setup my test/trial BTP account with Integration Suite / Process runtime. While my setup looks perfectly fine (have doublechecked all the steps), I still cannot create a new Integration Flow inside a package. When I press either 'Add' or 'Add and Open in Editor', nothing happens, no visual feedback (nor an error displayed) and iFlow is not created.

On the other hand, trying to open an existing (copied from pre-existing SAP content) iFlow, displays the following error:

"Error while loading the details of the integration flow. Try opening the artifact again. If the issue persists, report the incident to SAP."

I have checked the existing SAP notes and still cannot find anything meaningful to solve this.


Do you have any ideas ?
Appreciated,
Vil

entry.title: Error when opening Iflow in trial account
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting this error when opening iflow in trial account.

Should we create incident with sap even for trial account issue?

Error while loading the details of the integration flow. Try opening the artifact again. If the issue persists, report the incident to SAP
